channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Colorado two-way star Travis Hunter wins Heisman Trophy as college football's top player
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Travis Hunter turned every play into prime time — on both sides of the ball — and ultimately took home the Heisman Trophy. Now he’s got a leg up on his celebrity coach at Colorado.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Storms encase Iowa and eastern Nebraska in ice and generate first tornado warning in San Francisco
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A major ice storm created treacherous driving conditions across Iowa and eastern Nebraska this weekend and prompted temporary closures of Interstate 80 after numerous cars and trucks slid off the road.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Donald Trump's presidential library to settle defamation lawsuit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — ABC News has agreed to pay $15 million toward Donald Trump’s presidential library to settle a defamation lawsuit over anchor George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that the president-elect had been found civilly liable for raping writer E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ABC settles Trump’s defamation suit for $15M
item.description: The settlement enables both the anchor and Trump to avoid sitting for court-ordered depositions next week.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Holiday shopping scams to avoid this winter
item.description: Waiting on a plethora of present-related notifications? Don't assume all emails and texts you get are legitimate.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, KRON4

item.title: South Korea’s parliament votes to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol over his martial law order
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s parliament on Saturday impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol over his stunning and short-lived martial law decree, a move that ended days of political paralysis but set up an intense debate over Yoon's fate, as jubilant crowds roared to celebrate another defiant m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: LANDOVER, Md. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Woman arrested for attempting to climb fence outside White House
item.description: A woman was taken into custody by the U.S. Secret Service and arrested on Saturday after she attempted to breach a fence installed outside of the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: VIDEO: San Francisco cafe employee takes down would-be thief
item.description: The video (above) shows a man wearing a black hoodie and mask walking into the cafe and trying to take an item from a customer dining inside.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: President-elect Trump is signaling he plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production, centralizing power within the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Jamie Foxx reportedly injured in altercation at Beverly Hills restaurant
item.description: Foxx, who turned 57 on Friday, was dining with family at Mr. Chow when patrons at a nearby table hurled rude and vulgar insults to the "In Living Color" and "Ray" star, a witness told TMZ.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Bob Fernandez, a 100-year-old Pearl Harbor survivor, dies peacefully at home 83 years after bombing
item.description: Bob Fernandez, a 100-year-old survivor of the 1941 Japanese bombing of Pearl Harbor, has died peacefully at his nephew's home in Lodi, California.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 ways to avoid a financial holiday hangover
item.description: The festivity of December is replaced all too quickly by the due dates of January, when the bills from holiday spending and travel arrive.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, ABC 4

item.title: Woman accusing Jay-Z and Sean 'Diddy' Combs of sexual assault acknowledges inconsistencies
item.description: A woman who accused rappers Jay-Z and Sean “Diddy” Combs of sexually assaulting her when she was 13 at an awards show after-party has acknowledged certain inconsistencies in her story, while her lawyer said he will continue to vet her claims.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of Santas and a few Grinches hit the streets for annual SantaCon bar crawls
item.description: Thousands of Kris Kringles and jolly Old St. Nicks are jamming streets, sidewalks and bars from Adelaide to Vancouver on Saturday for annual SantaCon charity pub crawls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Isak Andic, founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, dies in accident, aged 71
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Isak Andic, the founder of Spanish fashion brand Mango, has died after suffering an accident, the company said on Saturday. He was 71.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi (D-Calif.), who was hospitalized during a trip to Luxembourg, underwent hip surgery early Saturday, according to a spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Obesity rate among US adults fell in 2023 for first time in over a decade: Study
item.description: The obesity rate among U.S. adults fell in 2023 for the first time in over a decade, according to a new study released on Friday. The study published in the journal JAMA Health Forum found that obesity nationwide has dropped from 46 percent in 2022 to 45.6 percent last year.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO shooter manhunt aided by San Francisco police tip
item.description: Days before Luigi Mangione was found after an officer in the Special Victims Unit of the San Francisco PD recognized Mangione in photos.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ingle, NewsNation

item.title: Woman who accused Duke lacrosse players of rape admits she lied
item.description: A woman who accused three former Duke University men’s lacrosse players of rape nearly two decades ago admitted she lied about the allegations and asked them for forgiveness.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KTLA 5

item.title: Border Report Live: 'Border czar' accepts Texas land as others push back on deportations
item.description: Tom Homan told Dr. Phil he's already working on a plan for borderlands the Texas offered the Trump administration's "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Fernie Ortiz, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration, matching Meta
item.description: E-commerce giant Amazon is planning to donate $1 million to President-elect Donald Trump’s inaugural fund, matching the amount Meta already donated to the incoming president’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Dick Vitale says he's cancer-free after 4 bouts with disease in just over 3 years
item.description: Vitale had surgery in the summer to remove cancerous lymph nodes from his neck. He was previously treated for melanoma and lymphoma, and had radiation treatments last year for vocal cord cancer.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly half of US teens are online 'constantly': Pew report
item.description: Nearly half of American teenagers say they are online “constantly” despite concerns about the effects of social media and smartphones on their mental health, according to a new report published Thursday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former inmate where Luigi Mangione is: Big cases 'upset' order
item.description: During the phone interview, current inmates shouted through the fence at the State Correctional Institution Huntingdon in Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: New Jersey drones: Sheriff calls for state of emergency
item.description: Shaun Golden, the sheriff of Monmouth County, New Jersey, says that more government assistance is needed to identify the drones.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: O’Malley: Calling Social Security a 'legal Ponzi scheme' is bull
item.description: "Social Security is not on a path for bankruptcy or insolvency," O'Malley said during a Thursday appearance on "The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department, Louisville reach deal after probe prompted by Breonna Taylor killing
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — The U.S. Justice Department and the city of Louisville have reached an agreement to reform the city’s police force after an investigation prompted by the fatal police shooting of Breonna Taylor and police treatment of protesters, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Recall issued for 2.6 million travel mugs over burn risk
item.description: Stanley has announced a recall of approximately 2.6 million Switchback and Trigger Action travel mugs, citing a burn hazard. The recall, which was issued in cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Kayaker who faked his death, fled the US charged after returning to Wisconsin
item.description: Police had previously said they believed Borgwardt planned his Aug. 12 disappearance and tried to conceal his efforts to mislead investigations.
item.dc:creator: Indiana Schilz, ABC 4
